name: The Wounded Rider
trigger: quest:greyvale_anomaly:stage == 0 AND player_location == accord_market_square AND session_time >= 300
description: A rider crashes through the market gates — dust-caked, one arm in a sling, horse half-dead. The market falls silent. He's shouting about creatures in the Greyvale, about Millhaven, about lights in the ruins. He collapses before he finishes.

dm_instructions.mood: sudden shift from warm normalcy to alarm
dm_instructions.pacing: the market noise drops to silence in a single beat
key_beats: The sound of hooves on cobblestone, too fast, too desperate
The market noise cuts off — vendors stop mid-word, children go quiet
The rider's voice, cracking: Hollow creatures in the Greyvale. Dozens. The Ashmark has moved south.
He falls from the horse. Someone catches him. Blood on the cobblestones.
The mood of the market shifts permanently — this is the moment the world stops feeling safe

name: Hollow in the Fields
description: The birdsong stops. Your companion tenses. Something is moving in the tree line — shapes that don't match the landscape, silhouettes that bend where shadows shouldn't.

dm_instructions.mood: tension building to dread to combat
dm_instructions.pacing: slow approach, the silence comes first, then the wrongness
key_beats: Natural sounds fade — birds first, then insects, then wind
The companion goes rigid: 'Do you hear that? That... silence?'
Movement at the tree line — shapes that hurt to look at directly
The Hollow creatures emerge: one Rend leading two Drifts
Combat begins — the creatures make sounds that have no source

name: The Ruins Stir
description: The discovery in the ruins has consequences. The Hollow corruption in the Greyvale intensifies. Reports reach the Accord. Factions take notice.

dm_instructions.mood: spreading unease, the sense that something has been set in motion
key_beats: The hum in the ruins grows louder — audible from the surface now
Hollow creatures increase in the northern Greyvale
Word reaches the Accord: the situation is escalating
Faction representatives begin asking questions

name: Competing Interests
description: Word has spread about what the player found in the ruins. Different factions want the artifact, the information, or both. The player must navigate competing interests.

dm_instructions.mood: political pressure, the sense of being watched and wanted
dm_instructions.pacing: faction representatives approach separately, each with their own framing
key_beats: A temple investigator (Valdris) wants the artifact entered into evidence
A Veythar archivist (Theron) wants to 'study it properly' — too eagerly
A Syrath operative (Nyx) wants to know everything, offers nothing in return
An Aelora guild master (Dara) wants to know if trade routes are safe
The player's choices here affect faction reputation permanently

name: Divine Recognition
description: The player's patron deity speaks directly to them about the discovery. The god's perspective reframes what the player found — and hints at a larger significance.

dm_instructions.mood: awe, weight of divine attention, the world holding its breath
dm_instructions.pacing: atmospheric shift first, then the voice, then silence
key_beats: The air thickens. Sound stops — not fades, stops.
A presence fills the space. Ancient. Immense. Not hostile, but overwhelming.
The god speaks — two sentences, weighted with millennia of perspective
Silence returns like a wave breaking. The world resumes.
The companion looks shaken but says nothing unless spoken to

name: The Hollow Advances
description: The Hollow incursion in the Greyvale intensifies. Millhaven is under direct threat. The disturbance at the ruins may have accelerated the corruption. The town needs defenders.

dm_instructions.mood: urgency, the stakes are now personal — people the player knows are in danger
key_beats: Word reaches the player: Millhaven is under siege
Elder Yanna has refused to evacuate
The fortification choices from Session 2 async matter now
The player must return and participate in a coordinated defense

name: A Simple Errand
trigger: session == 1 AND player_location == accord_market_square AND quest:greyvale_anomaly:not_started
description: A merchant asks the player for a small favor — find a missing delivery, settle a dispute with a vendor, help carry crates. Simple. Normal. The kind of thing that happens in a city that doesn't know it's about to be afraid.

dm_instructions.mood: warm, ordinary, the world working as it should
dm_instructions.pacing: unhurried, let the player explore and interact naturally
key_beats: A vendor or passerby engages the player casually
A simple task: find something, talk to someone, help with something
The player learns basic navigation and interaction
The companion offers commentary — light, relaxed, showing personality
The market feels alive, safe, worth protecting

name: Meeting Kael
trigger: session == 1 AND player_location IN [accord_market_square, accord_guild_hall] AND companion_not_met
description: The player meets their starting companion. Class-appropriate framing brings them together naturally.

dm_instructions.mood: casual meeting that becomes something more
key_beats: The companion is already present — sitting at a table, leaning against a wall, watching the crowd
Something brings them together: a shared reaction, a practical need, simple proximity
Brief conversation: the companion is guarded but not hostile
A moment of connection — humor, shared observation, mutual interest
The companion falls into step beside the player. It feels natural.
